presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
The documents address complementary aspects of access governance: one outlines the evidentiary requirements and gaps in Q1 vendor access reviews, while the other specifies the conditions and oversight mechanisms for privileged account approvals. Together, they reflect a structured but incomplete control environment where procedural compliance must be validated against actual evidence.

- Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) documents Q1 2024 vendor-control evidence and identifies gaps requiring resolution.  
- Missing EV-002 approval email must be recovered to close the control.  
- Privileged access is granted conditionally, with defined scope, logging, and quarterly review requirements.  
- Governance mandates vendor confirmation and archival of evidence for audit readiness.  
- Evidence collection is synthetically modeled, limiting direct operational applicability.  
- Control effectiveness depends on reconciling procedural design with actual documented proof.
key_highlights: The documents address complementary aspects of access governance: one outlines the evidentiary requirements and gaps in Q1 vendor access reviews, while the other specifies the conditions and oversight mechanisms for privileged account approvals. Together, they reflect a structured but incomplete control environment where procedural compliance must be validated against actual evidence.
Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) documents Q1 2024 vendor-control evidence and identifies gaps requiring resolution.
Missing EV-002 approval email must be recovered to close the control.
Privileged access is granted conditionally, with defined scope, logging, and quarterly review requirements.
Governance mandates vendor confirmation and archival of evidence for audit readiness.
Evidence collection is synthetically modeled, limiting direct operational applicability.
